page: OVHcloud b2-7 is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel processor based on the Haswell architecture without TSX running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 2 vCPUs. It is equipped with 7.0 GB of RAM, yielding 3.5 GB of memory per core, and includes 50 GB of local SSD storage. Network capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 0 Gbps and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tiers. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1072.92, which falls in the bottom 25 percent, while the multi-core performance score is 2156.87, placing it in the bottom 10 percent. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 178.89 GB/sec and a large block uncached speed of 25.07 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25 percent. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds are also in the bottom 10 percent, reflecting the older CPU architecture and lack of hyperthreading.

In terms of workload execution, the server achieves 3.11 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 2.84 GB/sec, both in the bottom 25 percent. Geekbench and Passmark scores consistently rank in the bottom 10 percent to 25 percent across software compiling, image processing, and database operations. Given these performance metrics and the dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose tasks, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale database operations where high-throughput compute is not required.
description: OVHcloud b2-7 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring an x86_64 Intel Haswell processor running at 2.0 GHz. The instance provides 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, 7.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark results place this server in the lower performance tiers, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance in the bottom 25 percent and multi-core performance in the bottom 10 percent. Memory bandwidth is also modest, with large block write speeds at 19.33 GB/sec. It delivers 3.11 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 2.84 GB/sec in static web serving. Lacking GPU acceleration and high-speed network baselines, the b2-7 is designed for lightweight workloads, development environments, and basic web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-7 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server featuring 2 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs, 7.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage, designed for lightweight workloads and development.

tagline: An entry-level virtual server with dedicated Intel cores and local SSD storage for lightweight workloads and development environments.

7.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local SSD storage
KVM hypervisor virtualization with 1 complimentary public IPv4 address
Suitable for lightweight web serving and development environments
Delivers 3.11 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
